title: Sun Vanishes in Australian Sky
story: The sky went dark in Exeter, Western Australia as the moon slid in front of the sun. For nearly a minute, day turned to night during a total solar eclipse. Then, just as quickly, sunlight returned.

title: The Curies' Glowing Discovery
story: Imagine a substance that glows in the dark! On April 20, 1902, scientists Marie and Pierre Curie made an amazing breakthrough. The Curies' hard work in their laboratory led to important advances in understanding radioactivity, which later helped develop medical treatments.

title: Pasteur's Groundbreaking Milk Discovery
story: Louis Pasteur and Claude Bernard made a splash in science on April 20, 1862. These clever Frenchmen finished the first-ever pasteurization test. They found a way to make milk safer by heating it just right. This process killed harmful germs without changing milk's taste. Pasteur's method soon spread worldwide, helping keep people healthy when drinking milk and other foods.
activity_question: What did Pasteur and Bernard discover a way to make safer?
bonus_fact: Pasteurization is named after Louis Pasteur and is still used today to keep many drinks safe.

title: Racing Legend Phil Hill Zooms into Life
story: Vroom! The engines of racing history roared to life when Phil Hill was born in 1927. This future speed demon would grow up to become America's first Formula 1 world champion in 1961. Hill didn't just conquer F1; he also clinched victory at the grueling 24 Hours of Le Mans race three times. From his April 20 start in Miami, Florida, to checkered flags worldwide, Phil Hill's journey was truly legendary.
activity_question: What major racing achievement did Phil Hill accomplish in 1961?
activity_choices: Became America's first Formula 1 world champion
Won his first 24 Hours of Le Mans race
Set a new speed record in Miami
Invented a new type of racing engine
activity_answer: Became America's first Formula 1 world champion
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous endurance race besides the 24 Hours of Le Mans?

title: Flying High: Amsterdam to Bonaire
story: Soaring across the Atlantic, a plane made history in 1991. On April 20, the first non-stop flight from Amsterdam's Schiphol Airport landed at Flamingo Airport in Bonaire, a sunny Caribbean island. This long journey connected Europe directly to the tropical paradise, opening new possibilities for travelers and islanders alike.
activity_question: Where did the historic flight land?
follow_up_question: Why do you think non-stop flights are important for travelers?

title: Canada's Space Leap: ANIK A2 Takes Flight
story: Zoom! On April 20, 1973, Canada rocketed into the space age. The ANIK A2 blasted off, becoming the first commercial satellite to orbit Earth. This metal marvel helped connect far-flung communities across Canada's vast wilderness. Suddenly, phone calls and TV shows could reach the most remote corners of the country!
activity_question: What did the ANIK A2 become?
activity_choices: The first commercial satellite in orbit
The first maple leaf in space
activity_answer: The first commercial satellite in orbit
bonus_fact: ANIK means 'little brother' in Inuktitut, an Indigenous language of Canada.

title: A World's Fair Extravaganza in Spain
story: Colorful flags waved as Expo '92 burst to life in Seville, Spain. This massive world's fair celebrated the 500th anniversary of Columbus's voyage to the Americas. Visitors marveled at futuristic pavilions and cutting-edge technology from countries around the globe. The event, which opened on April 20, 1992, drew millions of curious explorers to experience the wonders of human achievement.
activity_question: What anniversary did Expo '92 celebrate?
follow_up_question: What do you think was the coolest thing to see at a world's fair?

title: Cruise Ships Find Safe Harbor
story: Floating ghost towns no more! On April 20, 2020, the last three cruise ships still at sea during the COVID pandemic finally reached dry land. After weeks of uncertainty, these massive floating hotels docked safely in Marseille, Barcelona, and Los Angeles. Passengers and crew breathed sighs of relief as their unexpected extended voyages came to an end.
activity_question: Where did the three cruise ships dock?
activity_choices: New York, Miami, and San Francisco
follow_up_question: Why do you think these ships stayed at sea longer than others?
